title: cxxrtl: add a way to observe state changes during the commit step
body:
The commit observer is a structure containing a callback that is invoked whenever the `commit()` method changes a wire or a memory. This allows code external to the compiled netlist to react to changes in the design state in a very efficient way. One example of how this feature can be used is an efficient implementation of record/replay.

Note that the VCD writer does not benefit from this feature because it must be able to react to changes in any debug items and not just those that contain design state.

<hr>

There is a performance penalty associating with translating wire/memory identity (`base` argument) to some sort of persistent identifier in an upper layer using this feature. The penalty, specific to this translation, can be as high as 25% compared to an uninstrumented design. At a later point, the argument to the observer may end up being switched from a base pointer to a permanently assigned integer, but there are challenges associated with that design and the current one is much simpler while still providing adequate performance.
